result.summary: Discord exposes a public developer docs site plus authenticated REST API, Gateway realtime API, and webhook execution; authentication uses bot tokens, user OAuth2 access tokens, OAuth2 client credentials, or webhook URLs.

discord_oauth_client.label: Discord developer application OAuth2 client
discord_oauth_client.setup: Create a developer application in the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications). From the application settings, copy your `client_id` and `client_secret`. Use these to perform Discord OAuth2 flows, including authorization code and client credentials, according to the [OAuth2 docs](https://docs.discord.com/developers/topics/oauth2).

discord_bot_token.label: Discord bot token
discord_bot_token.setup: In the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications), open your application, add a bot user on the Bot tab, and copy the bot token shown there. Use it as the `Authorization: Bot <token>` credential for bot-authenticated API calls and Gateway connections.
